summary: Matched Roy's ftemblem fighter file by replacing asm/melee/ft/chara/ftemblem.s with C source and a header, then updating build lists to use src/melee/ft/chara/ftemblem.o. The implementation covers Roy death/load callbacks plus several item/visibility or model-state helper routines around held item checks, accessory toggles, and calls into shared fighter helpers. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftemblem.c with C implementations for ftRoy_OnDeath, ftRoy_OnLoad, func_8014EF60, func_8014F040, func_8014F088, func_8014F0D0, func_8014F148, func_8014F168, and func_8014F1AC.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftemblem.h with include guards, HSD_GObj/Fighter includes, and prototypes for the newly matched functions.
Changed obj_files.mk so ftemblem builds from $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftemblem.o instead of the asm object.
Added ftemblem.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.

Promoted func_80136474 and func_801364E8 declarations in include/functions.h from unknown commented placeholders to typed prototypes: void func_80136474(struct _Fighter*) and void func_801364E8(struct _HSD_GObj*).
smart_moves: Kept the asm symbol names for still-unnamed functions while giving the known callbacks their fighter-specific names, ftRoy_OnDeath and ftRoy_OnLoad.
Introduced a local ftemblem.h instead of leaving prototypes scattered or implicit.
Used existing Fighter fields exposed by the project, notably gobj->user_data, x1974_heldItem, and x222C, to express the assembly behavior clearly.
Preserved the assembly switch shape in func_8014EF60 with explicit cases 1 through 4 mapping to func_80070FB4 values 1, 0, 2, and 3.
Matched the small floating-point helper routines directly with literal constants 3.0f and 0.0f, replacing sdata2 labels lbl_804D9E60 and lbl_804D9E64.
Updated both normal object lists and epilogue-unscheduled lists, which is necessary when migrating an asm file to C in this build setup.
decomp_lessons: When converting a fighter character asm file to C, remember to remove the asm object from obj_files.mk and replace it with the corresponding src object.
If the new C file calls previously unknown shared routines, add typed prototypes in include/functions.h based on observed argument types rather than relying on commented unknown declarations.
For fighter callbacks, gobj->user_data can often be typed as Fighter* and used to replace raw 0x2C loads from HSD_GObj.
Known Fighter struct fields can remove magic offsets: the asm write to 0x222C became ft->x222C = 0, and loads from 0x1974 became ft->x1974_heldItem.
Simple sdata2 float constants in asm may be expressible as ordinary float literals when the generated code still matches.

Small wrapper callbacks can often be matched directly as one-line calls once callees have correct prototypes, as shown by ftRoy_OnLoad calling func_80136474(gobj->user_data) and func_8014F148 calling func_801364E8(gobj).
naming_conventions: Roy-specific lifecycle callbacks use the ftRoy_ prefix: ftRoy_OnDeath and ftRoy_OnLoad.
The character module keeps the filename/module name ftemblem for Roy, matching the pre-existing asm path.
Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as func_8014EF60, func_8014F040, and func_8014F1AC.
Header guard style used here is lowercase with surrounding underscores: _ftemblem_h_.
Fighter field names in this code use offset-prefixed names where semantic names are not fully known, e.g. x222C and x1974_heldItem.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translate repeated raw calls in death callbacks directly: ftRoy_OnDeath calls func_80074A4C for accessory or resource slots 0, 1, and 2 with values 0, 0, and -1, then clears Fighter::x222C.
Use an if around func_8026B2B4(ft->x1974_heldItem) == 0 to mirror the assembly's early exit when the held item state check is nonzero.
Represent the assembly branch ladder in func_8014EF60 as a switch over func_8026B320(ft->x1974_heldItem), with no default behavior needed beyond falling through.
Preserve arg1 conditional behavior after the switch: func_8014EF60 conditionally calls func_80070C48(gobj, 1), while func_8014F0D0 conditionally calls func_80070CC4(gobj, 1).
Small guard functions func_8014F040 and func_8014F088 share the pattern of checking func_8026B2B4 on the held item before calling func_80070CC4 or func_80070C48 with argument 0.
The two float-setting functions match paired calls to func_800704F0 for indices 1 and 0 using the same float literal.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_8014EF60 or other ftemblem helper functions.
Search for other fighter files using func_80070FB4, func_80070C48, func_80070CC4, or func_800704F0 to infer better names and shared behavior.
Check later struct work for semantic names of Fighter::x222C and held-item related behavior around x1974_heldItem.
Look for later declarations or definitions of func_80136474 and func_801364E8 to confirm their true names and signatures.
